engineIntelligence.h2: Suzuki Every Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Suzuki Every in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data for JDM import models.

cards.altText: Suzuki Every K6A engine - petrol 658cc replacement UK
knownFailures: Valve seal failure - blue smoke on cold startup, common after 60k miles on high-mileage K6A units.
Distributor pickup coil failure - causes intermittent no-start or stalling, well-documented on UK owner forums.
Timing chain stretch - rattling noise from front cover, can lead to catastrophic failure if ignored.

cards.altText: Suzuki Every R06A engine - petrol 658cc replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger failure on turbo variants - oil starvation or wastegate sticking reported on higher-mileage imports.
Injector coking - rough idle and loss of power, more common on short-journey urban use examples.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,100-£2,900 (supply only) Estimated from comparable R06A units - actual quotes may vary due to newer model scarcity.

cards.altText: Suzuki Every F6A engine - petrol 657cc replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - overheating on older carburetted units, especially if coolant maintenance was neglected.
Carburettor wear - rough running and poor fuel economy on high-mileage pre-injection examples.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £750-£1,900 (supply only) Estimated from comparable F6A units - actual quotes may vary due to classic model scarcity.

commonProblems.h2: Suzuki Every Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Suzuki Every's K6A and R06A petrol engines are generally robust for Kei-class units, but valve seal wear, distributor failures and timing chain stretch drive most UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your import Every.

problems.h4: Valve Seal Failure - Why Suzuki Every K6A Engines Smoke on Startup
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Every Van DA64V, Every Pickup DA65T, Every 4WD (1999-2015, K6A engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Hardened valve stem seals allow oil to seep into combustion chambers overnight, causing blue smoke on cold startup - a well-documented K6A weakness on UK owner forums.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace valve stem seals, reassemble with new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: May resolve smoke for 12-24 months if no other wear; not viable if guides are worn

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K6A with upgraded seals, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; recommended if engine has other high-mileage wear or smoke persists

problems.recommendation: If your Every has under 70k miles and no other issues, valve seal replacement can be cost-effective. However, if the engine has high mileage, timing chain noise, or oil consumption, a rebuilt K6A with upgraded components offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K6A engine prices for your Suzuki Every

problems.h4: Distributor/Pickup Coil Failure - Why Suzuki Every Engines Won't Start
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Every K6A variants (1999-2015), especially early DA64V Van and DA65T Pickup
problems.rootCause: The K6A's distributor-mounted pickup coil degrades with heat and vibration, causing intermittent spark loss - a known weak point on UK import examples.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace distributor pickup coil and cap/rotor, test ignition timing
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves no-start if coil is sole fault; does not address other ignition wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K6A with new ignition components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if engine has other issues or coil failure is recurrent due to underlying problems

problems.recommendation: Pickup coil replacement is usually the right first step for a no-start Every with good compression. However, if the engine also shows timing chain noise, oil consumption, or has high mileage, investing in a rebuilt unit avoids repeated breakdowns.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Suzuki Every K6A ignition repair or replacement

problems.h4: Timing Chain Stretch - Why Suzuki Every Engines Rattle and Fail
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Every K6A and R06A variants (1999-2020), especially turbo models under load
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioners wear and chains stretch on high-mileage Kei engines, causing rattling noise and potential valve/piston interference if the chain skips teeth.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides and seals; requires front engine disassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Can extend life 20-40k miles if no other wear; not viable if valves are already damaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new timing components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if chain failure caused valve damage; best value for high-mileage engines

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling but the engine still runs smoothly, a timing chain kit replacement can be preventative. If the engine has skipped timing, suffered loss of power, or has high mileage, replacement is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K6A/R06A engine prices for your Suzuki Every

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Why Suzuki Every Turbo Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Every K6A-Turbo and R06A-Turbo variants (1999-2020), especially high-mileage imports
problems.rootCause: Turbo bearings wear from oil starvation or coking, causing boost loss, blue smoke under load, or complete turbo seizure - common on short-journey urban use Every turbos.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, rebuild with new bearings/seals or fit reconditioned unit, test boost
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves boost loss if engine internals are healthy; does not address underlying oiling issues

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Turbo K6A/R06A Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt turbo engine with upgraded turbo, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure caused oil contamination or engine has other high-mileage wear

problems.recommendation: If your Every turbo has failed but the engine still compresses well and has no smoke at idle, a turbo rebuild can be cost-effective. If there's blue smoke, oil contamination, or high mileage, a rebuilt turbo engine offers better reliability and warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Suzuki Every turbo engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Suzuki Every
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Every suffers valve seal smoke, distributor no-starts, timing chain rattle or turbo failure, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Suzuki Every specialists who understand JDM Kei engines. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get your import van back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Suzuki Every Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Suzuki Every and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Suzuki Every Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Suzuki Every's production timeline for UK imports, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Early Every: F6A Carburetted & Injection Engines
years.description: The earliest Suzuki Every imports to the UK used the 657cc F6A engine, now primarily sought by classic Kei van restorers.
keyChanges: Introduction of F6A-SOHC and F6A-DOHC variants
Transition from carburetted to early electronic fuel injection
Every Van and Pickup body styles established
knownFor: Simple, repairable design favoured by restorers
Carburettor wear causing rough running on high-mileage units
Head gasket failures if overheating occurred
Parts availability more limited than newer K6A/R06A
years.cta: Get quotes for 1990-1998 Suzuki Every replacement engines

years.description: The K6A 658cc DOHC engine became the standard for Suzuki Every imports, powering the majority of UK replacement enquiries today.
keyChanges: Introduction of K6A 658cc DOHC engine replacing F6A
Turbo and naturally aspirated variants offered
DA64V Van and DA65T Pickup chassis codes established
knownFor: Valve seal failure causing blue smoke on cold startup - #1 K6A issue
Distributor pickup coil failures leading to intermittent no-start
Timing chain stretch on high-mileage units
Excellent fuel economy (40-50 mpg) for Kei-class vans
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2015 Suzuki Every replacement engines
years.ticker: Suzuki Every blue smoke on startup • Suzuki Every no start distributor • Suzuki Every timing chain rattle • Suzuki Every K6A valve seals
enquiries: Suzuki Every blue smoke on startup

years.description: The newer R06A engine with VVT and improved emissions began appearing in later Suzuki Every imports, now entering the UK replacement market.
keyChanges: Introduction of R06A 658cc engine with VVT technology
Improved emissions compliance for newer imports
Turbo variants retained with updated turbocharger design
mainEngines: R06A - 658cc petrol NA with VVT (Every Van/Pickup 2015-2020)
R06A-Turbo - 658cc petrol turbo with VVT (Every Turbo variants 2015-2020)
knownFor: Improved fuel efficiency and lower emissions vs K6A
Turbocharger failures on higher-mileage turbo variants
Injector coking on short-journey urban use examples
Growing replacement demand as these imports age into service

faq.h2: Suzuki Every Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is the Suzuki Every K6A engine reliable?
items.answer: The K6A is generally reliable for a Kei engine but has known weaknesses: valve seal wear causing blue smoke, distributor pickup coil failures, and timing chain stretch on high-mileage units. Regular maintenance extends life significantly.
keyPoints: Valve seal failure is the most common K6A issue after 60k miles
Distributor pickup coils fail intermittently causing no-start
Timing chain stretch requires preventative attention on high mileage
Oil changes with correct grade critical for longevity
items.cta: Compare rebuilt K6A engine prices for your Suzuki Every

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Suzuki Every engine?
items.answer: Typical UK rebuilt Suzuki Every engine costs range from £850-£2,900 supply-only depending on engine code (K6A/R06A/F6A), variant and supplier. Supply & fit adds £400-£800 labour.
keyPoints: K6A rebuilt: £850-£2,400 supply only
R06A rebuilt: £1,100-£2,900 supply only (newer model scarcity)
F6A rebuilt: £750-£1,900 supply only (classic model)
Supply & fit labour typically adds £400-£800
items.cta: Get quotes for Suzuki Every engine replacement cost UK

items.question: What causes blue smoke on startup in my Suzuki Every?
items.answer: Blue smoke on cold startup in Suzuki Every K6A engines is almost always valve stem seal failure - a well-documented issue where hardened seals allow oil into combustion chambers overnight.
keyPoints: Valve seals harden with age and heat cycles
Smoke appears only on cold startup, clears as engine warms
Repair involves cylinder head removal and seal replacement
Rebuilt engines include upgraded seals as standard
items.cta: Compare Suzuki Every valve seal repair vs replacement costs

items.question: Will a UK garage fit my Suzuki Every import engine?
items.answer: Many mainstream UK garages are unfamiliar with JDM Kei engines, but our network includes specialists experienced with Suzuki Every K6A/R06A fitment, wiring and ECU compatibility for import models.
keyPoints: JDM Every engines use different wiring/ECU vs UK Suzuki models
Specialist knowledge required for proper fitment and diagnostics
Our network garages understand Kei engine specifics
Supply & fit option available nationwide through vetted specialists
items.cta: Find Suzuki Every engine supply & fit specialists near me

items.question: Can I use a Suzuki Alto K6A engine in my Every?
items.answer: While both use the K6A code, Suzuki Every and Alto K6A engines have different mounting points, ancillaries and ECU calibrations - direct swaps are not recommended without significant modification.
keyPoints: Every K6A has unique engine mounts and accessory brackets
ECU and wiring harness differ between Every and Alto applications
Turbo/NA variants have different internal specifications
Always source an Every-specific K6A for direct replacement
items.cta: Get quotes for Suzuki Every-specific K6A engine replacement

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Suzuki Every?
items.answer: If your Every is otherwise in good condition and has sentimental or commercial value, a rebuilt K6A/R06A engine at £850-£2,900 often makes more sense than scrapping a functional import van.
keyPoints: Compare replacement cost vs vehicle market value
Rebuilt engines include 12-month warranty for peace of mind
Every imports are scarce in UK - replacement preserves asset
Consider supply & fit option to avoid finding a willing garage
items.cta: Compare Suzuki Every engine replacement vs vehicle value

items.question: How long does a Suzuki Every engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, Suzuki Every K6A/R06A engines can reach 80,000-120,000 miles. Valve seal wear typically appears around 60k miles but doesn't necessarily mean full replacement is needed immediately.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes with correct grade critical for longevity
Valve seals often need attention around 60k miles
Timing chain inspection recommended at 70k+ miles
Many UK Every imports exceed 100k miles with care
items.cta: Get maintenance advice for your Suzuki Every engine

Used engines: typically 3-6 months, verify before purchase

items.question: How do I identify which engine code is in my Suzuki Every?
items.answer: The engine code stamp is located on the engine block near the cylinder head on K6A/R06A units. Enter your registration above for instant DVLA verification of your Every's exact engine code and variant.
keyPoints: K6A stamp visible on front of block near timing cover
R06A has similar location but different casting marks
DVLA lookup via registration confirms engine code instantly
Chassis plate in engine bay also lists engine specification
